questions.prompt: When DNSFilter blocks a query for an HTTPS site, the user always sees the customised hosted block page.
questions.explanation: For HTTPS, the browser tries to negotiate TLS with the IP DNSFilter returns. Because the block-page server can't present a valid certificate for the user's intended domain, most modern browsers show a TLS error instead of the block page. This is intrinsic to DNS filtering.

questions.prompt: DNSFilter's hosted block page still supports a Bypass Password feature in 2026.
questions.explanation: DNSFilter removed the Bypass Password feature in late 2024 because it lifted every category and threat block for the entire browser session. The supported paths now are Allow-list entries and the block page's embedded notice form.
